hooks.command:
f=$(jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // .tool_response.filePath // empty'); [ -z "$f" ] && exit 0; echo "$f" | grep -qE 'templates/.*\.html$' || exit 0; jq -nc '{hookSpecificOutput:{hookEventName:"PostToolUse",additionalContext:"<system-reminder>You just edited a Django template. Two non-negotiable steps before reporting this change complete:

1. INVOKE THE frontend-design SKILL via the Skill tool if not already loaded this session. It carries the project-specific UI rules (skip-link, h1, empty-state shape, button hierarchy, mobile reflow at 320px, Bootstrap Icons, WCAG 2.2 AA).

2. CAPTURE A PLAYWRIGHT SCREENSHOT at 1280x900 desktop AND 320x800 mobile against the running dev stack, then read the images and visually verify the rendering. Playwright DOM-only assertions are not sufficient for UI changes (this exact gap shipped a 0-byte vendor CSS file recently).

This reminder fires on every template edit. If you have already loaded the skill and verified visually for this change, ignore.</system-reminder>"}}'
